rebatesSection.label: Rebate Programs
rebatesSection.heading: Stack Three Rebate Programs Together
rebatesSection.paragraph: Most LA homeowners qualify for all three. We confirm your eligibility during your free in-home estimate and submit every application on your behalf.

rebates.label: SoCalGas Rebate
rebates.desc: Available for qualifying high-efficiency tankless gas water heaters installed in your home.

rebates.label: Federal Tax Credit
rebates.desc: Inflation Reduction Act energy-efficient home improvement credit on qualifying ENERGY STAR units.

rebates.label: LADWP Rebate
rebates.desc: Los Angeles Department of Water & Power rebate for qualifying high-efficiency residential installations.

rebatesSection.totalParagraph: Off your new tankless water heater install. Most LA homeowners qualify — call us to find out exactly how much you'll save.

whyTanklessSection.label: Why Tankless
whyTanklessSection.heading: More Than Rebates — Long-Term Savings
whyTanklessSection.paragraph: Tankless units pay you back twice: once on the rebate, then every month on your gas bill. Most LA homeowners save 25–35% on water heating costs and never run out of hot water again.
bullets: Up to 35% lower monthly gas bills
Endless hot water — never run out
Full manufacturer warranty registered for you
whyTanklessSection.savingsDisclaimer: *Estimated savings vs. a standard tank water heater for an average LA household. Actual savings vary.

faqs.q: How much can I actually save with rebates?
faqs.a: Most LA homeowners qualify for between $1,200 and $1,900 in combined rebates and tax credits when installing a high-efficiency tankless unit. The exact amount depends on your utility, the unit you choose, and your tax situation.

faqs.q: Do you handle the paperwork for me?
faqs.a: Yes — we handle every rebate application from start to finish. We submit the SoCalGas and LADWP forms, provide IRS-ready documentation for the federal tax credit, and follow up until you've received your check.
Most ENERGY STAR-certified tankless gas water heaters from Navien, Rinnai, Noritz, and Takagi qualify. We'll recommend the unit that maximizes your rebate eligibility based on your home.

faqs.q: How long does it take to receive the rebate?
faqs.a: Utility rebates typically arrive within 6–10 weeks after installation. Federal tax credits are claimed when you file your taxes for the year the install was completed.
Yes. The federal tax credit stacks on top of utility rebates, so most homeowners claim two or three programs together. We'll show you exactly which ones you qualify for during your free estimate.

faqs.q: What if I'm replacing an existing tankless?
faqs.a: Rebates typically apply to new installs and tank-to-tankless conversions, not direct tankless-to-tankless replacements. We'll confirm eligibility before you commit.

finalCTA.headline: Ready to claim your $1,900 in rebates?
finalCTA.subheadline: Free in-home estimate. We confirm your exact rebate amount before any work begins. Same-day install available.
